Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Development of processing strategies for multigraded selective laser melting of Ti6Al4V and IN718

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V gas atomized powder (LPW, Runcorn, United Kingdom) was used. Powder size distribution was D10:23 μm, D50:33 μm, D90:46 μm. Spherical in shape. Layer thickness was fixed at 50 μm and the focal position of the laser beam was fixed on the powder bed. Laser power, scan speed and hatch distance were varied. Hatch distance 50–100 μm, Laser power 200–300–400 W, Scan speed 500–1900 mm/s. Energy density input range was 21–320 J/mm3. Full densification threshold observed for Ti6Al4V is 120 J/mm3. An inertization procedure was carried out with Ar purging. Base plates used were 12 mm thick Ti6Al4V plates. Representative defect-free condition at E = 178 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM of gas-atomized Ti6Al4V powder (D50=33 μm) on Ti6Al4V substrate, layer thickness 50 μm, Ar atmosphere, energy density range 21–320 J/mm3, full densification at E ≥ 120 J/mm3, representative condition E = 178 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: Optical microscopy analysis revealed that the microstructure of the components was composed of fine acicular α' grains. The α' martensitic laths are organised within prior β grain boundaries with different inclinations, mainly at ~ ±45° with respect to the build direction. The columnar grains are the result of epitaxial growth of the previous layer upon successive laser scans. Some lack of fusion defects could also be identified.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fine acicular α' martensitic laths within prior columnar β grain boundaries, epitaxial growth, laths inclined ~±45° to build direction. Some lack-of-fusion defects observed.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: 500 gf load, 15 s dwell time, defect-free specimen at E = 178 J/mm3

Process_Text.original: IN718 gas atomized powder (LPW, Runcorn, United Kingdom) was used. Powder size distribution was D10:18 μm, D50:30 μm, D90:47 μm. Spherical in shape. Layer thickness was fixed at 50 μm and the focal position of the laser beam was fixed on the powder bed. Hatch distance 50–100 μm, Laser power 250–350–450 W, Scan speed 100–1000 mm/s. Energy density input range was 63–900 J/mm3. Full densification threshold observed for IN718 is 250 J/mm3. Above 500 J/mm3 the process was not feasible for geometrical inaccuracy. An inertization procedure was carried out with Ar purging. Base plates used were 3 mm thick IN718 plates. Representative defect-free condition at E = 300 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM of gas-atomized IN718 powder (D50=30 μm) on IN718 substrate, layer thickness 50 μm, Ar atmosphere, energy density range 63–900 J/mm3, full densification at E ≥ 250 J/mm3, representative condition E = 300 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: The typical layer by layer SLM manufacturing characteristics, as well as the melt pool morphology are clearly visible. Columnar grains across several layers can also be distinguished, with a very fine dendritic structure. The SEM micrograph shows a detail of the dendritic structure of the material, with evidence of strong segregation in the interdendritic zones, which resulted enriched in Nb and Mo. The bright phases, observable in the BSE image, achieve Nb content up to 23.7 wt% (measured by EDX analysis) and Mo up to 2.5%. According to literature, these high element contents can be associated to the formation of Ni3Nb δ–phase in the segregated areas.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Columnar grains with fine dendritic structure across layers. Strong Nb and Mo segregation in interdendritic zones (Nb up to 23.7 wt%, Mo up to 2.5% by EDX). Possible Ni3Nb δ-phase formation in segregated areas.

Microstructure_Info.Main_Phase: γ-Ni (FCC) with Ni3Nb δ-phase in segregated zones

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: 500 gf load, 15 s dwell time, defect-free specimen at E = 300 J/mm3

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V and IN718 powders were mixed in the desired ratios (10 wt% IN718) with the innovative powder mixer unit of the open SLM platform. Layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, focal position 0 mm, Ar atmosphere. Laser power 300–450 W, scan speed 350–850 mm/s, energy density input range 141–400 J/mm3. Two replicates for each condition were produced. Representative defect-free condition at E = 165 J/mm3. Base plates used were 12 mm thick Ti6Al4V plates.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM of premixed 10 wt% IN718 in Ti6Al4V powder blend, layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, Ar, energy density range 141–400 J/mm3, representative defect-free condition at E = 165 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: The SEM micrographs belonging to 10 wt% IN718 blend shows a non-uniform microstructure across the specimen surface. The deposited layers are clearly distinguishable and no alpha martensitic lathes could be observed. The alloying elements which compose IN718 (mainly Ni, Cr and Fe), act as beta-stabilizers for titanium, promoting the growth and retaining of this phase down to room temperature. The dendritic microstructure is not clearly observable within the layers, indicating a low microsegregation level. Instead, the presence of macrosegregated areas located at the layer interfaces is clearly evidenced. In these areas, Ni reaches values up to 15 wt% and Cr up to 3.6 wt%.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Non-uniform β-stabilized microstructure, no α' martensite. Layers clearly distinguishable. Low microsegregation within layers but macrosegregation at layer interfaces (Ni up to 15 wt%, Cr up to 3.6 wt%). No porosity, no cracks.

Microstructure_Info.Main_Phase: β-Ti (retained by Ni, Cr, Fe β-stabilizers)

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: 500 gf load, 15 s dwell time, defect-free specimen at E = 165 J/mm3

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V and IN718 powders were mixed in the desired ratios (20 wt% IN718) with the innovative powder mixer unit. Layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, focal position 0 mm, Ar atmosphere. Laser power 300–450 W, scan speed 350–850 mm/s, energy density input range 141–400 J/mm3. 20 wt% IN718 blended alloy could be successfully produced without cracks with E > 266 J/mm3. Representative defect-free condition at E = 267 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM of premixed 20 wt% IN718 in Ti6Al4V blend, layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, Ar, crack-free above E > 266 J/mm3, representative condition E = 267 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: SEM analysis reveals a complex (yet uniform, as stated) microstructure, with clear evidence of a second phase nucleated in the interdendritic zones. The formation of β phase is expected with the increase of IN718 content. SEM/EDX analysis puts in to evidence that the second phase is rich in Ni (up to 17% wt).
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Complex but uniform microstructure. β phase formed. Second phase nucleated in interdendritic zones, rich in Ni (up to 17 wt%). No porosity.

Microstructure_Info.Main_Phase: β-Ti with Ni-rich second phase in interdendritic zones

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: 500 gf load, 15 s dwell time, defect-free specimen at E = 267 J/mm3

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V and IN718 powders were mixed in the desired ratios (30 wt% IN718). Layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, focal position 0 mm, Ar atmosphere. Laser power 300–450 W, scan speed 350–850 mm/s, energy density input range 141–400 J/mm3. All specimens with 30 wt% IN718 exhibited severe macro cracks, regardless of the process parameters. Specimens produced with high energy density input exhibited less and smaller cracks. Representative condition at E = 400 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM of premixed 30 wt% IN718 in Ti6Al4V blend, layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, Ar. Severe macro cracks in all conditions. Representative condition E = 400 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: The further IN718 fraction increase, in the 30 wt% IN718 blend, promotes the nucleation of the intermetallic Ti2Ni phase (bright phase in SEM). It can be expected that the α-Ti + Ti2Ni eutectoid transformation starts at grain boundaries of the β phase. Due to the massive precipitation of the intermetallic phase the dendritic solidification microstructure is no more observable. The size of the cracks with the 30 wt% IN718 are comparable to the size of the specimens. Thermodynamic calculations predict Ti2Ni starts to form in the liquid phase for 30 wt% IN718.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Ti2Ni intermetallic phase nucleation. α-Ti + Ti2Ni eutectoid transformation at β grain boundaries. Dendritic structure no longer observable due to massive intermetallic precipitation. Severe macro cracks (specimen-scale). Ti2Ni forms in liquid phase per thermodynamic calculation.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: 500 gf load, 15 s dwell time, specimen at E = 400 J/mm3 (cracked specimen)

Process_Text.original: Ti6Al4V and IN718 powders were mixed in the desired ratios (40 wt% IN718). Layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, focal position 0 mm, Ar atmosphere. Laser power 300–450 W, scan speed 350–850 mm/s, energy density input range 141–400 J/mm3. All specimens with 40 wt% IN718 exhibited severe macro cracks, regardless of the process parameters. Representative condition at E = 400 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM of premixed 40 wt% IN718 in Ti6Al4V blend, layer thickness 50 μm, hatch distance 50 μm, Ar. Severe macro cracks in all conditions. Representative condition E = 400 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: The 40 wt% IN718 blend promotes the nucleation of the intermetallic Ti2Ni phase (bright phase in SEM). The α-Ti + Ti2Ni eutectoid transformation starts at grain boundaries of the β phase. Due to the massive precipitation of the intermetallic phase the dendritic solidification microstructure is no more observable. Thermodynamic calculations predict the progressive destabilization of α-phase with temperature formation decreased to values lower than 600 °C for the 40% blend. Ti2Ni starts to form in the liquid phase for 40 wt% IN718.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Ti2Ni intermetallic nucleation. α-phase destabilized (formation temperature < 600 °C). Massive intermetallic precipitation obliterates dendritic structure. Ti2Ni forms in liquid phase. Severe macro cracks.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: 500 gf load, 15 s dwell time, specimen at E = 400 J/mm3 (cracked specimen)

Composition_Info.Alloy_Name_Raw: Graded Ti6Al4V/IN718 - Ti6Al4V base layer (0 wt% IN718)

Process_Info.Process_Category: Multi-material Selective Laser Melting (SLM) - Graded Transition

Process_Text.original: The first material deposited was Ti6Al4V, for 42 layers. Laser power 300 W, scan speed 700 mm/s, hatch distance 50 μm, layer thickness 50 μm, energy density 170 J/mm3. Built on 12 mm thick Ti6Al4V substrate. Ar atmosphere.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM graded specimen base: 42 layers of pure Ti6Al4V, P=300W, v=700mm/s, h=50μm, z=50μm, E=170 J/mm3, Ar.

Microstructure_Text.original: Graded transition specimen cross sectioned. The internal section appeared fully dense and crack free. No delamination between layers could be observed, nor between the first layers and the Ti6Al4V substrate.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully dense, crack-free, no delamination at substrate interface.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Multi-material Selective Laser Melting (SLM) - Graded Transition

Process_Text.original: IN718 quantity was increased to 5 wt% for 12 layers. Laser power 300 W, scan speed 700 mm/s, hatch distance 50 μm, layer thickness 50 μm, energy density 170 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM graded step: 12 layers of 5 wt% IN718 blend, P=300W, v=700mm/s, h=50μm, z=50μm, E=170 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully dense and crack free internal section. No delamination between layers could be observed.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Multi-material Selective Laser Melting (SLM) - Graded Transition

Process_Text.original: IN718 quantity was increased to 10 wt% for 12 layers. Laser power 300 W, scan speed 700 mm/s, hatch distance 50 μm, layer thickness 50 μm, energy density 170 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM graded step: 12 layers of 10 wt% IN718 blend, P=300W, v=700mm/s, h=50μm, z=50μm, E=170 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully dense and crack free. The decrease in Ti content along the build direction, together with the increased presence of IN718 alloying elements, is coherent with the graded deposition. The remelting effect induced by the laser beam contributes in creating a smoother transition from one blend to the other.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully dense, crack-free. Smooth compositional transition due to remelting and interdiffusion.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Multi-material Selective Laser Melting (SLM) - Graded Transition

Process_Text.original: IN718 quantity was increased to 15 wt% for 12 layers. Laser power 300 W, scan speed 550 mm/s, hatch distance 50 μm, layer thickness 50 μm, energy density 218 J/mm3.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM graded step: 12 layers of 15 wt% IN718 blend, P=300W, v=550mm/s, h=50μm, z=50μm, E=218 J/mm3.

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully dense and crack free. Smooth compositional transition observed. At height 3.62 mm, EDX reveals Ni-enriched zones (up to 15.36 wt% Ni in Region C). The highest hardness values are obtained in the layers strongly enriched in Ni, Nb, Cr and Fe content, presumably associated to the formation of hard intermetallic phases.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully dense, crack-free. Ni-enriched zones (up to 15.36 wt% Ni) detected by EDX. Hard intermetallic phase formation in enriched areas.

Process_Info.Process_Category: Multi-material Selective Laser Melting (SLM) - Graded Transition

Process_Text.original: IN718 quantity was increased to 20 wt% for 12 layers. Laser power 300 W, scan speed 450 mm/s, hatch distance 50 μm, layer thickness 50 μm, energy density 267 J/mm3. The graded specimen was successfully built up to the 20 wt% IN718 content.
Process_Text.simplified: SLM graded step: 12 layers of 20 wt% IN718 blend, P=300W, v=450mm/s, h=50μm, z=50μm, E=267 J/mm3. Successfully built, fully dense and crack-free.

Microstructure_Text.original: Fully dense and crack free. At height 4.10 mm, EDX reveals Ni-enriched zones up to 15.68 wt% Ni in Region C. The dilution of the Ti6Al4V composition, due to the layer interdiffusion, is also observable by the corresponding Cr, Fe and Nb increase and the reduction of Al and V.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Fully dense, crack-free. Compositional dilution via layer interdiffusion. Ni-enriched zones (up to 15.68 wt% Ni) detected by EDX.
